passage_title: The Search for the Anti-aging Pill

sentences.sentence: As researchers on aging noted recently, no treatment on the market today has been proved to slow human aging - the build-up of molecular and cellular damage that increases vulnerability to infirmity as we grow older.
sentences.translation: 正如衰老研究人员最近指出的，目前市场上没有任何治疗被证实能减缓人类衰老——也就是随着年龄增长，分子和细胞损伤累积、使人更易患病的过程。

structure.text: As researchers on aging noted recently
no treatment on the market today has been proved to slow human aging
the build-up of molecular and cellular damage that increases vulnerability to infirmity as we grow older
that increases vulnerability to infirmity as we grow older

difficult_points: as 引导来源说明，破折号后解释 human aging。
has been proved to slow 是完成时被动。
that 定语从句修饰 damage，as 从句说明年龄增长背景。

sentences.sentence: But one intervention, consumption of a low-calorie* yet nutritionally balanced diet, works incredibly well in a broad range of animals, increasing longevity and prolonging good health.
sentences.translation: 但一种干预措施——食用低热量且营养均衡的饮食——对多种动物都非常有效，能延长寿命和健康状态。

structure.text: one intervention, consumption of a low-calorie* yet nutritionally balanced diet
works incredibly well in a broad range of animals
increasing longevity and prolonging good health

difficult_points: 逗号中 consumption...是 intervention 的同位语。
increasing 和 prolonging 分词短语表示结果。

sentences.sentence: Those findings suggest that caloric restriction could delay aging and increase longevity in humans, too.
sentences.translation: 这些发现表明，热量限制也可能延缓人类衰老并延长寿命。

structure.text: Those findings
suggest that caloric restriction could delay aging and increase longevity in humans, too
that caloric restriction could delay aging and increase longevity in humans, too

difficult_points: that 从句作 suggest 的宾语。
could 说明对人类效果仍是可能性而非确定事实。

sentences.sentence: Unfortunately, for maximum benefit, people would probably have to reduce their caloric intake by roughly thirty percent, equivalent to dropping from 2,500 calories a day to 1,750.
sentences.translation: 遗憾的是，为获得最大益处，人们可能必须把热量摄入减少约30%，相当于从每天2500卡降至1750卡。

structure.text: Unfortunately, for maximum benefit
people would probably have to reduce their caloric intake by roughly thirty percent
equivalent to dropping from 2,500 calories a day to 1,750

difficult_points: would have to 表示假设下的必要性。
equivalent to dropping...解释百分比的实际含义。

sentences.sentence: Few mortals could stick to that harsh a regimen, especially for years on end.
sentences.translation: 很少有人能坚持如此严苛的方案，尤其是连续多年坚持。

structure.text: Few mortals
could stick to that harsh a regimen
especially for years on end

difficult_points: that harsh a regimen 是形容词前置的特殊结构，意为如此严苛的方案。
for years on end 表示连续多年。

sentences.sentence: But what if someone could create a pill that mimicked the physiological effects of eating less without actually forcing people to eat less?
sentences.translation: 但如果有人能制造一种模仿少吃生理效果、却不强迫人少吃的药片呢？

structure.text: what if someone could create a pill
that mimicked the physiological effects of eating less
without actually forcing people to eat less

difficult_points: what if 引导假设问句。
that 定语从句修饰 pill，without 后接动名词。

sentences.sentence: Could such a 'caloric-restriction mimetic', as we call it, enable people to stay healthy longer, postponing age-related disorders (such as diabetes, arteriosclerosis, heart disease and cancer) until very late in life?
sentences.translation: 这种我们称为“热量限制模拟物”的东西能否让人保持健康更久，把与年龄有关的疾病推迟到生命很晚阶段？

structure.text: Could such a 'caloric-restriction mimetic', as we call it, enable people to stay healthy longer
as we call it
postponing age-related disorders (such as diabetes, arteriosclerosis, heart disease and cancer) until very late in life

difficult_points: as we call it 是插入语。
postponing 分词短语说明 enable 的结果。

sentences.sentence: Scientists first posed this question in the mid-1990s, after researchers came upon a chemical agent that in rodents seemed to reproduce many of caloric restriction's benefits.
sentences.translation: 20世纪90年代中期，研究人员发现一种在啮齿动物中似乎能复制热量限制诸多益处的化学物质后，科学家首次提出了这个问题。

structure.text: Scientists first posed this question in the mid-1990s
after researchers came upon a chemical agent
that in rodents seemed to reproduce many of caloric restriction's benefits

difficult_points: after 从句说明提出问题的时间。
came upon 表示偶然发现，that 修饰 agent。

sentences.sentence: No compound that would safely achieve the same feat in people has been found yet, but the search has been informative and has fanned hope that caloric-restriction (CR) mimetics can indeed be developed eventually.
sentences.translation: 目前还没有发现能在人类身上安全实现同样效果的化合物，但这项搜索富有收获，也燃起了热量限制模拟物最终确实能够开发出来的希望。

structure.text: No compound that would safely achieve the same feat in people has been found yet
that would safely achieve the same feat in people
but the search has been informative and has fanned hope that caloric-restriction (CR) mimetics can indeed be developed eventually

difficult_points: that 定语从句修饰 compound。
has been found 是完成时被动，hope 后接 that 从句。

sentences.sentence: The benefits of caloric restriction
sentences.translation: 热量限制的益处。

structure.text: The benefits of caloric restriction

difficult_points: 这是段落标题，不是完整叙述句。
of caloric restriction 修饰 benefits。

sentences.sentence: The hunt for CR mimetics grew out of a desire to better understand caloric restriction's many effects on the body.
sentences.translation: 对热量限制模拟物的探索源于更好理解热量限制对身体诸多影响的愿望。

structure.text: The hunt for CR mimetics
grew out of a desire to better understand caloric restriction's many effects on the body

difficult_points: grow out of 表示源于。
to better understand 是 desire 的目的。

sentences.sentence: Scientists first recognized the value of the practice more than 60 years ago, when they found that rats fed a low-calorie diet lived longer on average than free-feeding rats and also had a reduced incidence of conditions that become increasingly common in old age.
sentences.translation: 60多年前，科学家首次认识到这种做法的价值：他们发现，吃低热量饮食的老鼠平均寿命比自由进食的老鼠长，老年常见疾病的发生率也更低。

structure.text: Scientists first recognized the value of the practice more than 60 years ago
when they found that rats fed a low-calorie diet lived longer on average than free-feeding rats and also had a reduced incidence of conditions
fed a low-calorie diet
that become increasingly common in old age

difficult_points: when 从句嵌套 found 后的 that 从句。
fed...修饰 rats，that 定语从句修饰 conditions。

sentences.sentence: What is more, some of the treated animals survived longer than the oldest-living animals in the control group, which means that the maximum lifespan (the oldest attainable age), not merely the normal lifespan, increased.
sentences.translation: 此外，一些接受处理的动物活得比对照组最长寿的动物还久，这意味着最大寿命而不仅是正常寿命增加了。

structure.text: What is more
some of the treated animals survived longer than the oldest-living animals in the control group
which means that the maximum lifespan (the oldest attainable age), not merely the normal lifespan, increased

difficult_points: which 指前面整个事实。
插入语解释 maximum lifespan；not merely 构成对比。

sentences.sentence: Various interventions, such as infection-fighting drugs, can increase a population's average survival time, but only approaches that slow the body's rate of aging will increase the maximum lifespan.
sentences.translation: 各种干预措施（如抗感染药物）可以增加群体平均生存时间，但只有减缓身体衰老速度的方法才能增加最大寿命。

structure.text: Various interventions, such as infection-fighting drugs, can increase a population's average survival time
but only approaches that slow the body's rate of aging will increase the maximum lifespan
that slow the body's rate of aging

difficult_points: such as 举例说明 interventions。
only approaches...强调必要条件，that 修饰 approaches。

sentences.sentence: The rat findings have been replicated many times and extended to creatures ranging from yeast to fruit flies, worms, fish, spiders, mice and hamsters.
sentences.translation: 老鼠研究结果被多次复制，并扩展到从酵母到果蝇、蠕虫、鱼、蜘蛛、老鼠和仓鼠等生物。

structure.text: The rat findings
have been replicated many times and extended to creatures ranging from yeast to fruit flies, worms, fish, spiders, mice and hamsters
ranging from yeast to fruit flies, worms, fish, spiders, mice and hamsters

difficult_points: 两个过去分词 replicated 和 extended 共用完成时被动。
ranging from...列举适用物种范围。

sentences.sentence: Until fairly recently, the studies were limited to short-lived creatures genetically distant from humans.
sentences.translation: 直到不久前，这些研究还局限于与人类基因距离较远的短命生物。

structure.text: Until fairly recently
the studies were limited to short-lived creatures genetically distant from humans
genetically distant from humans

difficult_points: were limited to 表示局限于。
genetically distant 修饰 creatures。

sentences.sentence: But caloric-restriction projects underway in two species more closely related to humans - rhesus and squirrel monkeys - have made scientists optimistic that CR mimetics could help people.
sentences.translation: 但在两种与人类亲缘更近的动物——恒河猴和松鼠猴——中进行的热量限制项目，使科学家乐观地认为热量限制模拟物可能帮助人类。

structure.text: caloric-restriction projects underway in two species more closely related to humans
rhesus and squirrel monkeys
have made scientists optimistic that CR mimetics could help people

difficult_points: underway 修饰 projects，表示正在进行。
make somebody optimistic that 是使役结构，that 从句说明乐观内容。

sentences.sentence: The monkey projects demonstrate that, compared with control animals that eat normally, caloric-restricted monkeys have lower body temperatures and levels of the pancreatic hormone insulin, and they retain more youthful levels of certain hormones that tend to fall with age.
sentences.translation: 猴子项目显示，与正常进食的对照动物相比，限制热量的猴子体温和胰岛素水平更低，并保持了某些随年龄下降的激素的更年轻水平。

structure.text: The monkey projects demonstrate that
compared with control animals that eat normally
caloric-restricted monkeys have lower body temperatures and levels of the pancreatic hormone insulin, and they retain more youthful levels of certain hormones
that tend to fall with age

difficult_points: compared with 短语作比较背景。
that 从句中 and 连接 have 与 retain。
that 定语从句修饰 hormones。

sentences.sentence: The caloric-restricted animals also look better on indicators of risk for age-related diseases.
sentences.translation: 限制热量的动物在年龄相关疾病风险指标上也表现更好。

structure.text: The caloric-restricted animals
also look better on indicators of risk for age-related diseases

difficult_points: look better on 表示在指标上表现更好。
age-related 修饰 diseases。

sentences.sentence: For example, they have lower blood pressure and triglyceride levels (signifying a decreased likelihood of heart disease), and they have more normal blood glucose levels (pointing to a reduced risk for diabetes, which is marked by unusually high blood glucose levels).
sentences.translation: 例如，它们血压和甘油三酯水平更低（意味着心脏病可能性下降），血糖水平也更正常（意味着糖尿病风险降低；糖尿病的特征是血糖异常升高）。

structure.text: For example, they have lower blood pressure and triglyceride levels
signifying a decreased likelihood of heart disease
and they have more normal blood glucose levels
pointing to a reduced risk for diabetes, which is marked by unusually high blood glucose levels
which is marked by unusually high blood glucose levels

difficult_points: 两个分词短语分别解释指标含义。
which 定语从句修饰 diabetes，说明其特征。

sentences.sentence: Further, it has recently been shown that rhesus monkeys kept on caloric-restricted diets for an extended time (nearly 15 years) have less chronic disease.
sentences.translation: 此外，最近研究显示，长期（近15年）接受热量限制饮食的恒河猴慢性疾病更少。

structure.text: Further
it has recently been shown
that rhesus monkeys kept on caloric-restricted diets for an extended time (nearly 15 years) have less chronic disease
kept on caloric-restricted diets for an extended time (nearly 15 years)

difficult_points: it 是形式主语，that 从句是真正内容。
kept on...修饰 monkeys。

sentences.sentence: They and the other monkeys must be followed still longer, however, to know whether low-calorie intake can increase both average and maximum lifespans in monkeys.
sentences.translation: 然而，还必须继续观察它们和其他猴子更长时间，才能知道低热量摄入是否能同时延长猴子的平均和最大寿命。

structure.text: They and the other monkeys must be followed still longer
however
to know whether low-calorie intake can increase both average and maximum lifespans in monkeys
whether low-calorie intake can increase both average and maximum lifespans in monkeys

difficult_points: must be followed 是必须被继续观察的被动结构。
to know 表目的，whether 从句是 know 的宾语。

sentences.sentence: Unlike the multitude of elixirs being touted as the latest anti-aging cure, CR mimetics would alter fundamental processes that underlie aging.
sentences.translation: 不同于被吹捧为最新抗衰老疗法的大量灵丹妙药，热量限制模拟物会改变衰老所依赖的基本过程。

structure.text: Unlike the multitude of elixirs being touted as the latest anti-aging cure
CR mimetics would alter fundamental processes
that underlie aging

difficult_points: being touted 是进行时被动，修饰 elixirs。
that 定语从句修饰 processes。

sentences.sentence: We aim to develop compounds that fool cells into activating maintenance and repair.
sentences.translation: 我们的目标是开发能诱使细胞启动维护和修复机制的化合物。

structure.text: We aim to develop compounds
that fool cells into activating maintenance and repair

difficult_points: aim to develop 表示目标。
fool somebody into doing 表示诱使某人做某事。

sentences.sentence: How a prototype caloric-restriction mimetic works
sentences.translation: 一种热量限制模拟物原型的工作原理。

structure.text: How a prototype caloric-restriction mimetic works

difficult_points: 这是段落标题，不是完整叙述句。
How 引导的从句说明工作方式。

sentences.sentence: The best-studied candidate for a caloric-restriction mimetic, 2DG (2-deoxy-D-glucose), works by interfering with the way cells process glucose.
sentences.translation: 研究最充分的热量限制模拟物候选物 2DG 通过干扰细胞处理葡萄糖的方式发挥作用。

structure.text: The best-studied candidate for a caloric-restriction mimetic, 2DG (2-deoxy-D-glucose)
works by interfering with the way cells process glucose
the way cells process glucose

difficult_points: 2DG 是 candidate 的同位语。
by interfering with 说明作用机制，the way 后接定语从句。

sentences.sentence: It has proved toxic at some doses in animals and so cannot be used in humans.
sentences.translation: 事实证明它在动物身上某些剂量下有毒，因此不能用于人类。

structure.text: It has proved toxic at some doses in animals
and so cannot be used in humans

difficult_points: has proved 表示事实证明。
so 连接毒性结果与不能用于人类的结论。

sentences.sentence: But it has demonstrated that chemicals can replicate the effects of caloric restriction; the trick is finding the right one.
sentences.translation: 但它证明了化学物质可以复制热量限制的效果；关键在于找到正确的化合物。

structure.text: it has demonstrated that chemicals can replicate the effects of caloric restriction
that chemicals can replicate the effects of caloric restriction
the trick is finding the right one

difficult_points: that 从句是 demonstrated 的宾语。
分号后 finding 动名词作表语，one 指正确的化合物。

sentences.sentence: Cells use the glucose from food to generate ATP (adenosine triphosphate), the molecule that powers many activities in the body.
sentences.translation: 细胞利用食物中的葡萄糖生成 ATP（三磷酸腺苷），这种分子为身体许多活动提供能量。

structure.text: Cells
use the glucose from food to generate ATP
the molecule that powers many activities in the body
that powers many activities in the body

difficult_points: use A to do 表示利用 A 做某事。
that 定语从句修饰 molecule。

sentences.sentence: By limiting food intake, caloric restriction minimizes the amount of glucose entering cells and decreases ATP generation.
sentences.translation: 通过限制食物摄入，热量限制减少进入细胞的葡萄糖量，并降低 ATP 生成。

structure.text: By limiting food intake
caloric restriction minimizes the amount of glucose entering cells and decreases ATP generation
entering cells

difficult_points: By limiting 表示实现效果的方式。
entering cells 修饰 glucose，两个谓语并列。

sentences.sentence: When 2DG is administered to animals that eat normally, glucose reaches cells in abundance but the drug prevents most of it from being processed and thus reduces ATP synthesis.
sentences.translation: 当 2DG 被给予正常进食的动物时，葡萄糖大量进入细胞，但药物阻止其中大部分被处理，从而降低 ATP 合成。

structure.text: When 2DG is administered to animals that eat normally
that eat normally
glucose reaches cells in abundance but the drug prevents most of it from being processed and thus reduces ATP synthesis
from being processed

difficult_points: When 从句中 that 修饰 animals。
prevents most of it from being processed 是被动不定式，it 指 glucose。
thus 表示结果。

sentences.sentence: Researchers have proposed several explanations for why interruption of glucose processing and ATP production might retard aging.
sentences.translation: 研究人员提出了几种解释，说明中断葡萄糖处理和 ATP 生成为何可能延缓衰老。

structure.text: Researchers have proposed several explanations
for why interruption of glucose processing and ATP production might retard aging

difficult_points: for why 引出 explanations 的具体内容。
might 表示推测，retard 表示延缓。

sentences.sentence: One possibility relates to the ATP-making machinery's emission of free radicals, which are thought to contribute to aging and to such age-related diseases as cancer by damaging cells.
sentences.translation: 一种可能性与 ATP 生成机制释放自由基有关；人们认为自由基会通过损伤细胞促成衰老和癌症等年龄相关疾病。

structure.text: One possibility relates to the ATP-making machinery's emission of free radicals
which are thought to contribute to aging and to such age-related diseases as cancer by damaging cells

difficult_points: which 指 free radicals。
are thought to contribute 是被动观点表达，by damaging 说明方式。

sentences.sentence: Reduced operation of the machinery should limit their production and thereby constrain the damage.
sentences.translation: 降低该机制的运作应能限制自由基生成，从而约束损伤。

structure.text: Reduced operation of the machinery
should limit their production and thereby constrain the damage

difficult_points: Reduced operation 是过去分词作定语，their 指 free radicals。
thereby 表示由此产生的结果。

sentences.sentence: Another hypothesis suggests that decreased processing of glucose could indicate to cells that food is scarce (even if it isn't) and induce them to shift into an anti-aging mode that emphasizes preservation of the organism over such 'luxuries' as growth and reproduction.
sentences.translation: 另一种假设认为，葡萄糖处理减少可能向细胞表明食物稀缺（即使事实并非如此），并诱导它们转入抗衰老模式，优先保存机体而不是生长和繁殖等“奢侈品”。

structure.text: Another hypothesis suggests that decreased processing of glucose could indicate to cells that food is scarce (even if it isn't) and induce them to shift into an anti-aging mode
that food is scarce (even if it isn't)
that emphasizes preservation of the organism over such 'luxuries' as growth and reproduction

difficult_points: suggests 后的 that 从句含 indicate 和 induce 两个并列动词。
even if it isn't 省略了 food is scarce。
that 定语从句修饰 mode，强调优先保存机体。
